001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.lang.reflect.*;
023import java.time.*;
024import java.time.Duration;
025import java.time.format.*;
026import java.time.temporal.*;
027import java.util.*;
028
029import javax.xml.datatype.*;
030
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swaps.*;
034
035/**
036 * Centralized ISO 8601 formatting and parsing utility for date/time and Duration types.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Provides the built-in serialization format for {@link Temporal}, {@link Calendar},
040 * {@link Date}, {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}, and {@link Duration} objects.
041 *
042 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
043 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
044 * </ul>
045 */
046public final class Iso8601Utils {
047
048   private Iso8601Utils() {}
049
050   private static final ZoneId Z = ZoneId.of("Z");
051
052   private static final DateTimeFormatter YEAR_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("uuuu");
053   private static final DateTimeFormatter YEAR_MONTH_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("uuuu-MM");
054
055   private static final Map<Class<?>, DateTimeFormatter> DEFAULT_FORMATTERS = Map.ofEntries(
056      Map.entry(Instant.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT),
057      Map.entry(ZonedDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME),
058      Map.entry(OffsetDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME),
059      Map.entry(LocalDate.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE),
060      Map.entry(LocalDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME),
061      Map.entry(LocalTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_TIME),
062      Map.entry(OffsetTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_TIME),
063      Map.entry(Year.class, YEAR_FORMATTER),
064      Map.entry(YearMonth.class, YEAR_MONTH_FORMATTER)
065   );
066
067   private static DatatypeFactory datatypeFactory;
068   static {
069      try {
070         datatypeFactory = DatatypeFactory.newInstance();
071      } catch (DatatypeConfigurationException e) {
072         throw toRex(e);
073      }
074   }
075
076   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
077   // Formatting
078   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
079
080   /**
081    * Formats a date/time or Duration value to its ISO 8601 string representation.
082    *
083    * <p>
084    * Convenience dispatcher that uses default per-type wire formats; callers that need format-aware
085    * output should use the per-type helpers directly:
086    * {@link #formatDate}, {@link #formatCalendar}, {@link #formatTemporal},
087    * {@link #formatDuration}, {@link #formatPeriod}.
088    *
089    * @param value The value to format.
090    * @param type The class metadata for the value (used for selecting the appropriate formatter for Temporal types).
091    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used when the value lacks zone info).
092    * @return The ISO 8601 string representation.
093    */
094   @SuppressWarnings({
095      "java:S1172" // type kept for API compatibility; future callers may use it for per-type dispatch hints
096   })
097   public static String format(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone) {
098      if (value instanceof Duration d)
099         return formatDuration(d, DurationFormat.ISO_8601_WITH_DAYS);
100      if (value instanceof Period p)
101         return formatPeriod(p, PeriodFormat.ISO_8601);
102      if (value instanceof Calendar || value instanceof XMLGregorianCalendar)
103         return formatCalendar(value, type, CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME, timeZone);
104      if (value instanceof Date d)
105         return formatDate(d, type, DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME, timeZone);
106      if (value instanceof TemporalAccessor t)
107         return formatTemporal(t, type, TemporalFormat.DEFAULT, timeZone);
108      return value.toString();
109   }
110
111   /**
112    * Formats a {@link Duration} value using the supplied wire format.
113    *
114    * @param value The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
115    * @param format The duration wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to {@link DurationFormat#ISO_8601_WITH_DAYS}).
116    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
117    */
118   public static String formatDuration(Duration value, DurationFormat format) {
119      if (value == null)
120         return null;
121      return (format == null ? DurationFormat.ISO_8601_WITH_DAYS : format).format(value);
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Formats a {@link Period} value using the supplied wire format.
126    *
127    * @param value The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
128    * @param format The period wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to {@link PeriodFormat#ISO_8601}).
129    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
130    */
131   public static String formatPeriod(Period value, PeriodFormat format) {
132      if (value == null)
133         return null;
134      return (format == null ? PeriodFormat.ISO_8601 : format).format(value);
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Formats a {@link Date} value using the supplied wire format.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * {@code java.sql.Date}, {@code java.sql.Time}, and {@code java.sql.Timestamp} subclasses all dispatch
142    * through {@link DateFormat#format(Date, ZoneId)} just like {@code java.util.Date}.
143    *
144    * @param value The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
145    * @param sourceType The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
146    * @param format The date wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to {@link DateFormat#ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME}).
147    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone).
148    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
149    */
150   @SuppressWarnings({
151      "java:S1172" // sourceType kept for API symmetry with parseDate; callers pass ClassMeta context for potential future use
152   })
153   public static String formatDate(Date value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, DateFormat format, TimeZone timeZone) {
154      if (value == null)
155         return null;
156      ZoneId zone = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
157      return (format == null ? DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME : format).format(value, zone);
158   }
159
160   /**
161    * Formats a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} value using the supplied wire format.
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} values always emit {@link XMLGregorianCalendar#toXMLFormat()} regardless
165    * of the configured {@code format} — mirroring the parse-side rule and the historical wire output.
166    * {@link Calendar} (including {@link GregorianCalendar}) values are formatted using the supplied
167    * {@link CalendarFormat}.
168    *
169    * @param value The value to format. Must be a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}.
170    *    <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
171    * @param sourceType The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
172    * @param format The calendar wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to {@link CalendarFormat#ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME}).
173    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone when the value lacks zone info).
174    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
175    */
176   @SuppressWarnings({
177      "java:S1172" // sourceType kept for API symmetry with parseCalendar; callers pass ClassMeta context for potential future use
178   })
179   public static String formatCalendar(Object value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, CalendarFormat format, TimeZone timeZone) {
180      if (value == null)
181         return null;
182      if (value instanceof XMLGregorianCalendar x)
183         return x.toXMLFormat();
184      ZoneId zone = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
185      return (format == null ? CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME : format).format((Calendar)value, zone);
186   }
187
188   /**
189    * Formats a {@link TemporalAccessor} value using the supplied wire format.
190    *
191    * @param value The value to format. Must be a {@link Temporal} subtype. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
192    * @param sourceType The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
193    * @param format The temporal wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to {@link TemporalFormat#DEFAULT}).
194    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone when the value lacks zone info).
195    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
196    */
197   @SuppressWarnings({
198      "java:S1172" // sourceType kept for API symmetry with parseTemporal; callers pass ClassMeta context for potential future use
199   })
200   public static String formatTemporal(TemporalAccessor value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, TemporalFormat format, TimeZone timeZone) {
201      if (value == null)
202         return null;
203      ZoneId zone = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
204      return (format == null ? TemporalFormat.DEFAULT : format).format(value, zone);
205   }
206
207   /**
208    * Formats a date/time value as an ISO date (date-only, for OpenAPI 'date' format).
209    *
210    * @param value The value to format.
211    * @param type The class metadata.
212    * @param timeZone The session time zone.
213    * @return The ISO date string.
214    */
215   @SuppressWarnings({
216      "java:S1172" // type kept for API compatibility; callers pass ClassMeta context for potential future use
217   })
218   public static String formatAsDate(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone) {
219      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
220      if (value instanceof Calendar c) {
221         ZonedDateTime zdt = (c instanceof GregorianCalendar gc) ? gc.toZonedDateTime() : c.toInstant().atZone(c.getTimeZone().toZoneId());
222         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE.format(zdt);
223      }
224      if (value instanceof Date d)
225         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE.format(d.toInstant().atZone(zoneId));
226      if (value instanceof Temporal t)
227         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE.format(ZonedDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(t, zoneId)));
228      return value.toString();
229   }
230
231   /**
232    * Formats a date/time value as an ISO date-time (for OpenAPI 'date-time' format).
233    *
234    * @param value The value to format.
235    * @param type The class metadata.
236    * @param timeZone The session time zone.
237    * @return The ISO date-time string.
238    */
239   @SuppressWarnings({
240      "java:S1172" // type kept for API compatibility; callers pass ClassMeta context for potential future use
241   })
242   public static String formatAsDateTime(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone) {
243      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
244      if (value instanceof Calendar c) {
245         ZonedDateTime zdt = (c instanceof GregorianCalendar gc) ? gc.toZonedDateTime() : c.toInstant().atZone(c.getTimeZone().toZoneId());
246         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(zdt.toInstant());
247      }
248      if (value instanceof Date d)
249         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(d.toInstant());
250      if (value instanceof Temporal t) {
251         ZonedDateTime zdt = ZonedDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(t, zoneId));
252         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(zdt.toInstant());
253      }
254      return value.toString();
255   }
256
257   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
258   // Parsing
259   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
260
261   /**
262    * Parses an ISO 8601 string into the specified date/time target type.
263    *
264    * <p>
265    * Dispatches to the appropriate per-type parser based on the target's {@link ClassMeta} category:
266    * {@link #parseDate parseDate}, {@link #parseCalendar parseCalendar}, {@link #parseTemporal parseTemporal},
267    * {@link #parseDuration parseDuration}, or {@link #parsePeriod parsePeriod} — each using its default
268    * wire format.
269    *
270    * @param <T> The target type.
271    * @param iso8601 The ISO 8601 string to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
272    * @param targetType The target class metadata.
273    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used for types that need a default zone).
274    * @return The parsed object, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code iso8601} is <jk>null</jk> or the target type is
275    *    not a recognized date/time/duration/period type.
276    */
277   @SuppressWarnings({
278      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for date/time type dispatch
279   })
280   public static <T> T parse(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TimeZone timeZone) {
281      if (iso8601 == null)
282         return null;
283      if (targetType.isDate())
284         return parseDate(iso8601, targetType, DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME, timeZone);
285      if (targetType.isCalendar())
286         return parseCalendar(iso8601, targetType, CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME, timeZone);
287      if (targetType.isTemporal())
288         return (T) parseTemporal(iso8601, (ClassMeta<? extends Temporal>)(ClassMeta<?>)targetType, TemporalFormat.DEFAULT, timeZone);
289      if (targetType.isDuration())
290         return (T) parseDuration(iso8601, DurationFormat.MILLIS);
291      if (targetType.isPeriod())
292         return (T) parsePeriod(iso8601, PeriodFormat.ISO_8601);
293      return null;
294   }
295
296   /**
297    * Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a {@link Duration}.
298    *
299    * <p>
300    * Handles ISO 8601 forms ({@code "PT1H"}, {@code "-PT30M"}, {@code "PT-6H"}, {@code "PT1.5S"}, …) as well
301    * as the bare-number wire forms produced by {@link DurationFormat#MILLIS}, {@link DurationFormat#SECONDS},
302    * {@link DurationFormat#NANOS}, and {@link DurationFormat#HOCON} (e.g. {@code "500ms"}, {@code "1.5s"}).
303    *
304    * <p>
305    * The {@code formatHint} disambiguates bare integer strings that could be milliseconds, nanos, or seconds —
306    * for all other numeric or ISO forms the input is self-describing and the hint is ignored.
307    *
308    * @param iso8601 The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> or empty returns <jk>null</jk>.
309    * @param formatHint The duration wire format used to disambiguate bare integer inputs. May be <jk>null</jk>
310    *    (defaults to {@link DurationFormat#MILLIS}).
311    * @return The parsed {@link Duration}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code iso8601} is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
312    */
313   public static Duration parseDuration(String iso8601, DurationFormat formatHint) {
314      if (iso8601 == null || iso8601.isEmpty())
315         return null;
316      String s = iso8601.trim();
317      // Strip optional surrounding quotes
318      if (s.length() >= 2 && s.startsWith("\"") && s.endsWith("\""))
319         s = s.substring(1, s.length() - 1).trim();
320      if (s.startsWith("PT-"))
321         s = "-PT" + s.substring(3);
322      // Number sniffing and explicit format hints.
323      if (isIntegerLiteral(s))
324         return (formatHint == null ? DurationFormat.MILLIS : formatHint).parse(s);
325      if (isDecimalLiteral(s))
326         return DurationFormat.SECONDS.parse(s);
327      if (isHoconLiteral(s))
328         return DurationFormat.HOCON.parse(s);
329      // Use manual parser for full control (handles PTnH, PTnM, PTn.nS, -PTnH, PT-6H, etc.)
330      Duration d = parseDurationManual(s);
331      if (d != null)
332         return d;
333      // Fallback to standard parser
334      try {
335         return Duration.parse(s);
336      } catch (Exception e) {
337         throw toRex(e);
338      }
339   }
340
341   /**
342    * Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a {@link Period}.
343    *
344    * <p>
345    * Handles standard ISO 8601 period strings ({@code "P1Y2M3D"}, …) as well as the bare-integer wire form
346    * produced by {@link PeriodFormat#DAYS}.
347    *
348    * @param value The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> or empty returns <jk>null</jk>.
349    * @param formatHint The period wire format used to interpret bare integers. May be <jk>null</jk>
350    *    (defaults to {@link PeriodFormat#DAYS}).
351    * @return The parsed {@link Period}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
352    */
353   public static Period parsePeriod(String value, PeriodFormat formatHint) {
354      if (value == null || value.isEmpty())
355         return null;
356      String s = value.trim();
357      if (isIntegerLiteral(s))
358         return (formatHint == null ? PeriodFormat.DAYS : formatHint).parse(s);
359      return Period.parse(s);
360   }
361
362   /**
363    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}.
364    *
365    * <p>
366    * Dispatches by target type:
367    * <ul>
368    *    <li>{@link XMLGregorianCalendar} targets always use {@link DatatypeFactory#newXMLGregorianCalendar(String)}
369    *       and ignore {@code formatHint}.
370    *    <li>{@link Calendar} / {@link GregorianCalendar} targets honor {@code formatHint} when set to anything
371    *       other than {@link CalendarFormat#NOT_SET} or {@link CalendarFormat#ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME}; the default
372    *       path uses content-based formatter autodetection.
373    * </ul>
374    *
375    * @param <T> The target type.
376    * @param iso8601 The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
377    * @param targetType The target class metadata. Must represent a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}.
378    * @param formatHint The calendar wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link CalendarFormat#NOT_SET}).
379    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
380    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code iso8601} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is not a
381    *    recognized calendar type.
382    */
383   @SuppressWarnings({
384      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for Calendar/XMLGregorianCalendar dispatch
385   })
386   public static <T> T parseCalendar(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, CalendarFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone) {
387      if (iso8601 == null)
388         return null;
389      Class<T> tc = targetType.inner();
390      if (XMLGregorianCalendar.class.isAssignableFrom(tc))
391         return (T) datatypeFactory.newXMLGregorianCalendar(iso8601);
392      if (! Calendar.class.isAssignableFrom(tc))
393         return null;
394      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
395      if (formatHint != null && formatHint != CalendarFormat.NOT_SET && formatHint != CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME)
396         return (T) formatHint.parse(iso8601, zoneId);
397      return (T) parseCalendarDefault(iso8601, zoneId);
398   }
399
400   /**
401    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a {@link Date}.
402    *
403    * <p>
404    * Honors {@code formatHint} when set to anything other than {@link DateFormat#NOT_SET} or
405    * {@link DateFormat#ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME}; the default path uses content-based formatter autodetection.
406    *
407    * <p>
408    * Only direct {@link Date} targets are produced — {@code java.sql.*} subclasses fall through to
409    * <jk>null</jk>, matching the original dispatch.
410    *
411    * @param <T> The target type.
412    * @param iso8601 The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
413    * @param targetType The target class metadata. Must represent {@link Date}.
414    * @param formatHint The date wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link DateFormat#NOT_SET}).
415    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
416    * @return The parsed {@link Date}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code iso8601} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is
417    *    not exactly {@code java.util.Date}.
418    */
419   @SuppressWarnings({
420      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for Date dispatch
421   })
422   public static <T> T parseDate(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, DateFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone) {
423      if (iso8601 == null)
424         return null;
425      if (targetType.inner() != Date.class)
426         return null;
427      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
428      if (formatHint != null && formatHint != DateFormat.NOT_SET && formatHint != DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME)
429         return (T) formatHint.parse(iso8601, zoneId);
430      return (T) parseDateDefault(iso8601, zoneId);
431   }
432
433   /**
434    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into the specified {@link Temporal} subtype.
435    *
436    * <p>
437    * Honors {@code formatHint} when set to anything other than {@link TemporalFormat#NOT_SET} or
438    * {@link TemporalFormat#DEFAULT}; the default path uses the per-subtype default formatter
439    * (e.g. {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_INSTANT} for {@link Instant}, {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE}
440    * for {@link LocalDate}, …).
441    *
442    * @param <T> The target temporal subtype.
443    * @param iso8601 The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
444    * @param targetType The target class metadata. Must represent a {@link Temporal} subtype.
445    * @param formatHint The temporal wire format. May be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link TemporalFormat#NOT_SET}).
446    * @param timeZone The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
447    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code iso8601} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is not a
448    *    {@link Temporal} subtype.
449    */
450   @SuppressWarnings({
451      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for per-subtype Temporal.from() dispatch
452   })
453   public static <T> T parseTemporal(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TemporalFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone) {
454      if (iso8601 == null)
455         return null;
456      Class<T> tc = targetType.inner();
457      if (! Temporal.class.isAssignableFrom(tc))
458         return null;
459      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
460      Class<? extends Temporal> ttc = (Class<? extends Temporal>) tc;
461      if (formatHint != null && formatHint != TemporalFormat.NOT_SET && formatHint != TemporalFormat.DEFAULT)
462         return (T) formatHint.parse(iso8601, ttc, zoneId);
463      return (T) parseTemporalDefault(iso8601, ttc, zoneId);
464   }
465
466   /**
467    * Hand-written state machine that parses the {@code PT}-prefixed body as a strict, gapless sequence of
468    * {@code number[whitespace]unit} components ({@code H}/{@code M}/{@code S}, case-insensitive).
469    *
470    * <p>
471    * Whitespace is permitted between a number and its unit and between/around components, but every
472    * non-whitespace character must belong to a valid component. The parser rejects (by returning <jk>null</jk>,
473    * the same signal used for any input it cannot handle) when it encounters stray characters, a malformed
474    * number (e.g. more than one decimal point, or a trailing dot with no fraction), or a number without a
475    * trailing unit. On rejection {@link #parseDuration} falls through to {@link Duration#parse(CharSequence)},
476    * which throws for genuinely invalid values. Numeric conversion uses the floating-point expression
477    * {@code (long)(value * 1_000_000_000)} to preserve historical rounding for well-formed inputs.
478    *
479    * @param s The candidate duration string (already trimmed/dequoted/PT-normalized by {@link #parseDuration}).
480    * @return The parsed {@link Duration}, or <jk>null</jk> if the string is too short, lacks a {@code PT}/{@code pt}
481    *    prefix, contains no component, or contains any stray/malformed content.
482    */
483   private static Duration parseDurationManual(String s) {
484      if (s == null || s.length() < 3)
485         return null;
486      boolean neg = s.startsWith("-");
487      String s2 = neg ? s.substring(1) : s;
488      if (!s2.startsWith("PT") && !s2.startsWith("pt"))
489         return null;
490      s2 = s2.substring(2);
491      long totalNanos = 0;
492      boolean found = false;
493      int len = s2.length();
494      int i = 0;
495      while (i < len) {
496         // Whitespace between/around components is skipped; any other non-component character is rejected.
497         if (isRegexWhitespace(s2.charAt(i))) {
498            i++;
499            continue;
500         }
501         // A component must begin with a digit; any other character is stray junk → reject.
502         if (!isAsciiDigit(s2.charAt(i)))
503            return null;
504         int start = i;
505         while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s2.charAt(i)))  // Integer part.
506            i++;
507         // Optional fraction: a single '.' that MUST be followed by at least one digit.
508         if (i < len && s2.charAt(i) == '.') {
509            i++;
510            if (i >= len || !isAsciiDigit(s2.charAt(i)))
511               return null;  // Trailing dot with no fraction → malformed.
512            while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s2.charAt(i)))
513               i++;
514         }
515         int numEnd = i;
516         while (i < len && isRegexWhitespace(s2.charAt(i)))  // Optional whitespace between number and unit.
517            i++;
518         // A unit designator is required; a number without one (or followed by stray chars) is malformed.
519         if (i >= len || !isDurationUnit(s2.charAt(i)))
520            return null;
521         found = true;
522         double v = Double.parseDouble(s2.substring(start, numEnd));
523         long nanos = (long)(v * 1_000_000_000);
524         char unit = Character.toUpperCase(s2.charAt(i));
525         switch (unit) {
526            case 'H': totalNanos += nanos * 3600; break;
527            case 'M': totalNanos += nanos * 60; break;
528            case 'S': totalNanos += nanos; break;
529            default: break;  // HTT: isDurationUnit guarantees H/M/S
530         }
531         i++;  // Consume the unit.
532      }
533      if (!found)
534         return null;
535      Duration d = Duration.ofNanos(totalNanos);
536      return neg ? d.negated() : d;
537   }
538
539   private static boolean isAsciiDigit(char c) {
540      return c >= '0' && c <= '9';
541   }
542
543   /**
544    * Matches the regex {@code \s} character class (without the {@code UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS} flag).
545    */
546   private static boolean isRegexWhitespace(char c) {
547      return c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\n' || c == '\u000B' || c == '\f' || c == '\r';
548   }
549
550   private static boolean isDurationUnit(char c) {
551      return c == 'H' || c == 'h' || c == 'M' || c == 'm' || c == 'S' || c == 's';
552   }
553
554   /**
555    * State-machine equivalent of {@code ^[+-]?\d++$} — an optional sign followed by one or more ASCII digits.
556    *
557    * @param s The string to test.
558    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the entire string is an optionally-signed integer literal.
559    */
560   private static boolean isIntegerLiteral(String s) {
561      int len = s.length();
562      int i = 0;
563      if (i < len && (s.charAt(i) == '+' || s.charAt(i) == '-'))
564         i++;
565      if (i == len)
566         return false;
567      for (int k = i; k < len; k++)
568         if (!isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(k)))
569            return false;
570      return true;
571   }
572
573   /**
574    * State-machine equivalent of {@code ^[+-]?\d++\.\d++$} — an optionally-signed decimal with digits on both
575    * sides of a single dot.
576    *
577    * @param s The string to test.
578    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the entire string is an optionally-signed decimal literal.
579    */
580   private static boolean isDecimalLiteral(String s) {
581      int len = s.length();
582      int i = 0;
583      if (i < len && (s.charAt(i) == '+' || s.charAt(i) == '-'))
584         i++;
585      int intStart = i;
586      while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(i)))
587         i++;
588      if (i == intStart)
589         return false;
590      if (i >= len || s.charAt(i) != '.')
591         return false;
592      i++;
593      int fracStart = i;
594      while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(i)))
595         i++;
596      if (i == fracStart)
597         return false;
598      return i == len;
599   }
600
601   /**
602    * State-machine equivalent of {@code ^[+-]?\d++(?:\.\d++)?(?:ns|us|ms|s|m|h|d)$} — an optionally-signed number
603    * (with optional fraction) followed by a HOCON time unit.
604    *
605    * @param s The string to test.
606    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the entire string is an optionally-signed HOCON duration literal.
607    */
608   private static boolean isHoconLiteral(String s) {
609      int len = s.length();
610      int i = 0;
611      if (i < len && (s.charAt(i) == '+' || s.charAt(i) == '-'))
612         i++;
613      int intStart = i;
614      while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(i)))
615         i++;
616      if (i == intStart)
617         return false;
618      // Optional fraction consumed only when '.' is followed by at least one digit; otherwise left for the unit.
619      if (i < len && s.charAt(i) == '.' && i + 1 < len && isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(i + 1))) {
620         i++;
621         while (i < len && isAsciiDigit(s.charAt(i)))
622            i++;
623      }
624      String unit = s.substring(i);
625      return unit.equals("ns") || unit.equals("us") || unit.equals("ms")
626         || unit.equals("s") || unit.equals("m") || unit.equals("h") || unit.equals("d");
627   }
628
629   private static Calendar parseCalendarDefault(String iso8601, ZoneId zoneId) {
630      var formatter = selectParserFormatter(iso8601);
631      var ta = new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(formatter.parse(iso8601), zoneId);
632      return GregorianCalendar.from(ZonedDateTime.from(ta));
633   }
634
635   private static Date parseDateDefault(String iso8601, ZoneId zoneId) {
636      var formatter = selectParserFormatter(iso8601);
637      var ta = new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(formatter.parse(iso8601), zoneId);
638      return Date.from(ZonedDateTime.from(ta).toInstant());
639   }
640
641   @SuppressWarnings({
642      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for Temporal.from() reflection
643   })
644   private static <T extends Temporal> T parseTemporalDefault(String iso8601, Class<T> tc, ZoneId zoneId) {
645      ZoneId offset = (tc == Instant.class) ? Z : zoneId;
646      var formatter = getFormatterForType(tc);
647      var ta = new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(formatter.parse(iso8601), offset);
648
649      try {
650         var parseMethod = info(tc).getPublicMethod(
651            x -> x.isStatic()
652               && x.isNotDeprecated()
653               && x.hasName("from")
654               && x.hasReturnType(tc)
655               && x.hasParameterTypes(TemporalAccessor.class)
656         ).map(MethodInfo::inner).orElse(null);
657
658         if (parseMethod != null)
659            return (T) parseMethod.invoke(null, ta);
660      } catch (IllegalAccessException | InvocationTargetException e) {
661         throw toRex(e);
662      }
663
664      throw new IllegalArgumentException("Cannot parse ISO 8601 string into type: " + tc.getName());
665   }
666
667   private static DateTimeFormatter getFormatterForType(Class<?> tc) {
668      var f = DEFAULT_FORMATTERS.get(tc);
669      return f != null ? f : DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT;
670   }
671
672   /**
673    * Auto-detects the appropriate parser formatter based on the string content.
674    */
675   private static DateTimeFormatter selectParserFormatter(String iso8601) {
676      boolean hasTime = iso8601.contains("T");
677      boolean hasZone = iso8601.endsWith("Z") || iso8601.contains("+")
678         || (iso8601.length() > 10 && iso8601.lastIndexOf('-') > 10);
679      if (hasTime && hasZone)
680         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME;
681      if (hasTime)
682         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME;
683      if (hasZone)
684         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE;
685      return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE;
686   }
687
688   /**
689    * Converts epoch milliseconds to the target date/time type.
690    *
691    * @param <T> The target type.
692    * @param epochMillis The epoch milliseconds value.
693    * @param targetType The target class metadata.
694    * @param timeZone The session time zone.
695    * @return The converted date/time object.
696    */
697   @SuppressWarnings({
698      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for date/time type conversion
699   })
700   public static <T> T fromEpochMillis(long epochMillis, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TimeZone timeZone) {
701      Class<T> tc = targetType.inner();
702      ZoneId zoneId = timeZone != null ? timeZone.toZoneId() : ZoneId.systemDefault();
703      Instant instant = Instant.ofEpochMilli(epochMillis);
704
705      if (tc == Instant.class) return (T) instant;
706      if (tc == ZonedDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId);
707      if (tc == OffsetDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toOffsetDateTime();
708      if (tc == LocalDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toLocalDateTime();
709      if (tc == LocalDate.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toLocalDate();
710      if (tc == LocalTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toLocalTime();
711      if (tc == OffsetTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toOffsetDateTime().toOffsetTime();
712      if (tc == Date.class) return (T) Date.from(instant);
713      if (Calendar.class.isAssignableFrom(tc)) {
714         var cal = GregorianCalendar.from(instant.atZone(zoneId));
715         return (T) cal;
716      }
717      if (XMLGregorianCalendar.class.isAssignableFrom(tc)) {
718         var gc = GregorianCalendar.from(instant.atZone(zoneId));
719         return (T) datatypeFactory.newXMLGregorianCalendar(gc);
720      }
721
722      return null;
723   }
724}
